patterns_recognized: F1* thin-sample asterisk + stress flags (confidence_downgrade/acute_roster) on sub-55% picks proved unreliable across 5/25-5/26: DET lost 10-6 on 5/26 matching this archetype exactly; treat as near-coin-flip regardless of corroborating PQI signal.

D-grade dual-signal-against (SP xERA against pick AND PQI against pick) holds ~60% directional accuracy in the archive — moderate negative signal applicable when collective BvP also opposes the pick side (5/5 WEAK_VS).

HARD CAP 6 edge-hallucination (>+25pp) has fired correctly on every application through 5/28; the calibrator cannot generate valid +33pp edge in MLB markets — no override warranted even when pre-cap grade is A.

Consecutive HOU@TEX series reversals (HOU won 9-0 on 5/25, TEX won 10-7 on 5/26) confirm this matchup is structurally volatile and unstakeable in the bottom-probability band — a third-game DOWNGRADE is the correct posture.

LAA @ DET.reasoning: D-grade with dual opposing signals: Rodriguez (LAA SP) holds a 0.60 xERA edge over Flaherty (DET SP) and PQI=-7.5 directly against DET's late-game profile. More critically, all 5 DET top-order batters are WEAK_VS Rodriguez (10 PA each, shrunk_OPS 0.618) — the strongest collective BvP opposition on today's slate. No fair_prob available removes market validation. D-grade dual-signal-against pattern holds ~60% directional accuracy in the archive; combined with the structural top-5 WEAK_VS BvP pattern, DOWNGRADE is warranted despite DET's F3 swing/take gap firing.
lessons_applied: D-grade dual-signal-against (SP+PQI against pick)
collective WEAK_VS BvP on pick side (5/5 batters, shrunk 0.618)
no-market-data caution on near-50% directional calls

MIN @ CHW.reasoning: Strong directional case for CHW: Davis Martin carries a 2.41 xERA advantage over Simeon Woods Richardson (10.55% K-rate, contact-oriented), with F3_swing_take_gap=4419.9 and PQI=+38.2 both confirming CHW. HARD CAP 7 barely fired (gap=+0.0009), consistent with near-zero threshold pattern that was direction-correct on 5/20. MIN batters vs Martin show shrunk_OPS 0.734 (MEANINGFUL, not threatening). CHW batters show shrunk 0.905 vs Woods Richardson (favorable). No market data but F1+F3+PQI triple-signal stack is unusually consistent at PLATINUM level.

ATL @ BOS.reasoning: Declining HARD CAP 10 rescue. BOS top-5 batters are all WEAK_VS Tolle (12 PA each, shrunk_OPS 0.633) while ATL carries an elite lineup (Acuña 0.907 OPS vs RHP, Olson 0.887 vs RHP). Fair_prob missing — no market validation for a 52.2% pick in the bottom-probability band. Sale holds a 0.86 xERA edge over Tolle which is the primary BOS support, but with BOS batters collectively suppressed vs Tolle and ATL's lineup depth, the directional case is insufficient to rescue HARD CAP 10. CONFIRM band hits only 47.6% in this range.
lessons_applied: HARD CAP 10 bottom-bucket — declining rescue without market validation

TOR @ BAL.reasoning: HARD CAP 6 correctly fires on edge_pp=+33.52pp (calibrator hallucination range; no override). Directionally, BAL is well-supported: Bassitt holds a 0.54 xERA edge over Corbin, PQI=+6.4 confirms, bullpen gap favors BAL (hl_bullpen_xwoba_gap=-0.0094). BAL's BvP is compelling — Pete Alonso has LOTS_OF_HISTORY vs Corbin (63 PA, 5 HR, shrunk_OPS 1.026, OWNER), Taylor Ward also OWNER (20 PA, shrunk 0.856). TOR has only Springer as OWNER vs Bassitt. Directional CONFIRM; HARD CAP 6 tier enforcement stands.

CHC @ PIT.reasoning: Paul Skenes carries a dominant xERA advantage (F1_xera_gap=4.25, SP edge 2.77) over Colin Rea; PQI=+43.7 strongly confirms PIT and f5_prob=0.769 reflects Skenes' F5 dominance. CHC BvP shows 2 OWNERs vs Skenes (Hoerner shrunk 0.813, Busch shrunk 0.858) — a mild caution, but Bryan Reynolds is a strong OWNER vs Rea for PIT (20 PA, 2 HR, shrunk_OPS 0.949). Skenes' elite-level xERA gap overrides the marginal CHC BvP concern.

HOU @ TEX.reasoning: Three converging DOWNGRADE signals: (1) F1_xera_gap=1.03* thin-sample asterisk with stress flags (confidence_downgrade=True, acute_roster) on pick_prob=0.518 — per 5/26 DET lesson, this archetype is near-coin-flip regardless of corroborating PQI. (2) HOU holds 2 OWNERs vs Eovaldi (Peña shrunk 0.800 OWNER, Alvarez shrunk 0.867 OWNER with 18 PA) — HOU's cleanup pair historically punishes TEX's SP. (3) Third consecutive HOU@TEX game with reversals on 5/25 (HOU won 9-0) and 5/26 (TEX won 10-7) — structurally volatile series. Withholding CONFIRM; HARD CAP 1 (negative edge) + HARD CAP 10 both correctly enforce.
